clean_title: feature request: allow permanently hiding problems through json configuration.
clean_body: there should be a [inline code] configuration setting to permanently exclude certain files from the problems panel. this should also prevent those problems from adding to the problem count. for example, i often want to exclude the node modules from problems, but don't want to consistently have that as a filter. example configuration: json { "problems.exclude": { " /node modules/ } }
clean_text: feature request: allow permanently hiding problems through json configuration. there should be a [inline code] configuration setting to permanently exclude certain files from the problems panel. this should also prevent those problems from adding to the problem count. for example, i often want to exclude the node modules from problems, but don't want to consistently have that as a filter. example configuration: json { "problems.exclude": { " /node modules/ } }

clean_title: token consumption loop
clean_body: type: bug i mistakenly assumed that the suggested /create-instructions command would generate a guidelines file for copilot. so i clarified that it should create the file based on the existing .cursorrules in my project. this was transformed into the following instruction: “follow instructions in create-instructions.prompt.md based on the file @.cursorrules.” this led to a prolonged drain of tokens and a stream of gibberish output, consuming 28% of my monthly allowance in a single request. it would drain even more if i didn't explicitly stop it. i acknowledge that this prompt didn't make any sense, but still, it's concerning that such loops might occur so easily. so i decided to share. i hope it helps to make the service better. extension version: 0.38.2 vs code version: code 1.110.1 ( [hash] , 2026-03-06t23:03:27.520z) os version: windows nt x64 10.0.26200 modes: system info |item|value| |cpus|amd ryzen 5 220 w/ radeon 740m graphics (12 x 3194)| |gpu status|2d canvas: enabled gpu0: vendor= 0x1002, device=0x1901 [amd radeon 740m graphics], driver vendor=amd, driver version=32.0.13062.3005 active gpu1: vendor= 0x1414, device=0x008c [microsoft basic render driver], driver version=10.0.26100.7309 machine model name: machine model version: direct rendering display compositor: disabled off ok gpu compositing: enabled multiple raster threads: enabled on opengl: enabled on rasterization: enabled raw draw: disabled off ok skia graphite: disabled off trees in viz: disabled off video decode: enabled video encode: enabled webgl: enabled webgl2: enabled webgpu: enabled webnn: disabled off| |load (avg)|undefined| |memory (system)|15.26gb (1.66gb free)| |process argv|--crash-reporter-id 613837d5-5653-4690-b5d4- [hash] | |screen reader|no| |vm|0%| a/b experiments [code block]

clean_title: sessions: ai code review doesn't allow running multiple times?
clean_body: if you run an ai code review on your changes, and then accept/reject the resulting comments, then the button for running a review seems to become disabled. in practice, i think it's pretty common for folks to run a couple passes of review--particularly with different models--just to make sure everything looks clean. and based on the current ux, i wasn't sure how to do that?
clean_text: sessions: ai code review doesn't allow running multiple times? if you run an ai code review on your changes, and then accept/reject the resulting comments, then the button for running a review seems to become disabled. in practice, i think it's pretty common for folks to run a couple passes of review--particularly with different models--just to make sure everything looks clean. and based on the current ux, i wasn't sure how to do that?

clean_title: sessions: allow providing instruction when converting a code review comment to agent feedback
clean_body: when you run an ai code review, there doesn't appear to be a way to add any instruction/clarification when accepting a piece of feedback? you can only accept comments and then submit them, which feels a little inflexible, when you want to discuss some feedback and/or accept partial feedback. so i wonder how we could allow the local ai review experience to be a bit more conversational, without overcomplicating the ux 🤔
clean_text: sessions: allow providing instruction when converting a code review comment to agent feedback when you run an ai code review, there doesn't appear to be a way to add any instruction/clarification when accepting a piece of feedback? you can only accept comments and then submit them, which feels a little inflexible, when you want to discuss some feedback and/or accept partial feedback. so i wonder how we could allow the local ai review experience to be a bit more conversational, without overcomplicating the ux 🤔

clean_title: terminal confused by input request
clean_body: not totally sure what happened here [f300c934.copilotmd]( [url] this was something like - it ran npx, asked for input - i clicked the button in chat to say 'y' - it asked another question that the agent can't answer anyway because it needs arrows/enter input. ("install with npm and start now?") got stuck on that [code block] - i took over and answered the question in the terminal - terminal call finished - now the agent is running more commands trying to answer the question - the terminal output is broken. i don't see any useful error messages anywhere about that - weird double-rendering of commands all over - have to consume a pru to get back on track

clean_title: ollama provider: misreading context length for some models
clean_body: no related issue found for this label and keywork "ollama" (open or not) --- description: i'm highlighting a bug happening for ollama provider, while fetching all the models, and getting detailed infos for all of them. when i add as provider my local ollama (currently having 7 cloud models pulled) i find that the models list reports a wrong/lower context length for some of the models (in particular, in those cases, it seems to be using the default value i've seen hardcoded in the [code involved]( [url] which is [inline code] resulting in the [inline code] text i see). as you can see here. but, if i follow manually the same workflow of the code, and fetch the get [inline code] , and then, for each model, use the "model" (the model code) to fetch post [inline code] passing the [inline code] in the body, i get the correct numbers. ( i'm referring to ollama local endpoints here ) while the code i've seen (the one in the link above) seems prepared to extract the [inline code] props to build the variable name of the context length prop, it doesn't seem to be working for some models. in fact, as you can see, other models in the list, report the correct value. basically, all the 4 models that report 33k in my screenshot, actually have a bigger (way bigger) context length. --- this is a problem, because when i then try to use the model for a big task, the extension trigger the [inline code] feature way to early, disrupting execution and context. --- env info: - copilot chat extension version: 0.39.1 - vs code version: 1.111.0 (commit: [hash] ) - os version: windows 11 (vscode about report [inline code] ) - feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): adding ollama provider - selected model (e.g. gpt 4.1, claude 3.7 sonnet): the problem comes before model usage. - logs: no relevant logs to share steps to reproduce: since the problem happen to me with only some models, i'm not sure about reproduction. let's try with the same cloud model. also, be aware that i'm not sure if you can pull :cloud models without a premium ollama plan. i would expect you can pull but not use... --- 1. install ollama locally 2. execute [inline code] (and eventually another from my screenshot that has a 33k context length for an a/b) 3. go to [inline code] page of the extension 4. add ollama provider with default values (unless your ollama instance is hosted differently) 5. check the context length column in the models table (you should spot some unexpected 33k) 6. verify the actual context length from your local ollama endpoints - get [url] (fetch all models list) [get the "model" field of the model. in this example [inline code] ] - post [url] with body [inline code] 7. realize that while the model specs, and the ollama api says the context length is [inline code] you are seeing 33k in the models list of the extension. 8. (optional) try to engage with one affected model in a long task or conversation, and see how the [inline code] is triggered when near to 33k tokens. this is the bigger problem, i think, from the user point of view. thanks! if you need more infos, i'm here 👍🏼

clean_title: subagent github rate-limit errors do not surface to the parent agent and do not show the "try again" action
clean_body: type: bug does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: n/a (copilot agent/subagent workflow) vs code version: unknown os version: macos steps to reproduce: 1. start a top-level copilot agent task that invokes a subagent. 2. have the subagent hit a github-side rate limit/error. 3. observe the parent/top-level conversation. expected: - the subagent failure should surface in the top-level conversation. - the same retry affordance (for example a "try again" button) should be available from the top-level context. actual: - the top-level conversation does not surface the rate-limit error. - no "try again" affordance appears at the top level. - the only way to see the failure is to open the subagent history and scroll to the bottom. additional notes: - a screenshot is available showing the nested subagent history error state. - this appears to be specifically about error propagation / retry ux from subagent - parent conversation, not just the existence of rate limiting itself.

clean_title: custom sub-agent lacks ability to use different models from agent.md model list
clean_body: if sub-agent hits a r-llmit (claude models constantly now) and fails and the main agent will just try to re-run the sub agent again (which of course fails again). meanwhile my agent.md looks like the below, which has multiple models in the model selection. even after explicitly telling the main agent to use the other model when calling the planner agent it continues with claude sonnet. --- name: planner description: researches the codebase and external docs, identifies edge cases, and produces implementation plans (no code). tools: ["read", "search", "web", "agent", "execute", "edit", "todo", "browser"] model: ["claude sonnet 4.6 (copilot)", "gpt-5.4 (copilot)"] target: vscode user-invocable: false --- doesn't even try to pass the model into the invocation shown here: metadata: requesttype : chatresponses model : gpt-5.4 maxprompttokens : 271997 maxresponsetokens: 128000 location : 7 otheroptions : {"stream":true,"store":false} reasoning : {"effort":"high","summary":"detailed"} intent : undefined starttime : 2026-03-17t15:30:13.253z endtime : 2026-03-17t15:30:28.576z duration : 15323ms ourrequestid : f50827ef-d34e-479e-94fe- [hash] requestid : f50827ef-d34e-479e-94fe- [hash] serverrequestid : f50827ef-d34e-479e-94fe- [hash] timetofirsttoken : 10370ms resolved model : gpt-5.4-2026-03-05 usage : {"prompt tokens":20987,"completion tokens":713,"total tokens":21700,"prompt tokens details":{"cached tokens":9856},"completion tokens details":{"reasoning tokens":516,"accepted prediction tokens":0,"rejected prediction tokens":0}} tools (4) : memory, manage todo list, runsubagent, task complete response: i’m invoking the exact [inline code] subagent on a tiny read-only task so you can inspect the request path in the debug logs; this tool interface does not expose a separate model selector, so i can control the agent name but not independently force a different backing model here. 🛠️ runsubagent (call axdsfsirjng4dzhl2mivahty) { "agentname": "planner", "description": "tiny planner ping", "prompt": "tiny read-only task for debug-log verification. do not write code. do not edit files. return a minimal execution graph with exactly one task that states you would inspect the repository root and confirm the presence of projectstate.md. keep the response concise and include all required planner fields for that one task: taskid, goal, dependson, canrunwith, exclusivefiles, allowedsharedfiles, sharedcontracts, contractowner, overlaprisk, agent, parallelgroup, verificationscope, donewhen, replanif." }

clean_title: unable to select a model using the model picker (ctrl+alt+period)
clean_body: type: bug 1. open chat. 2. notice that chat shows gemini 3 pro as the model. 3. open the model picker with ctrl+alt+period (.). 4. select gpt-5 mini. 5. frankly, select any other model. you can try using space or enter, neither one works. expected result: the model changes. steps taken to troubleshoot: confirming with reading the title after tabbing back into the input field. i also asked the chat what model it was using and it confirmed no change in model. sometimes the model picker does show a checkbox on the model itself, meaning it would be assumed it is selected but no confirmation is taking place and asking copilot chat confirms the older selection. this feels like a regression of sorts. vs code version: code - clean_text: unable to select a model using the model picker (ctrl+alt+period) type: bug 1. open chat. 2. notice that chat shows gemini 3 pro as the model. 3. open the model picker with ctrl+alt+period (.). 4. select gpt-5 mini. 5. frankly, select any other model. you can try using space or enter, neither one works. expected result: the model changes. steps taken to troubleshoot: confirming with reading the title after tabbing back into the input field. i also asked the chat what model it was using and it confirmed no change in model. sometimes the model picker does show a checkbox on the model itself, meaning it would be assumed it is selected but no confirmation is taking place and asking copilot chat confirms the older selection. this feels like a regression of sorts. vs code version: code - clean_title: sessions: worktree-init actions can feel a bit noisy after a while
clean_body: after configuring a run action to initialize worktrees, the integrated terminal will automatically open every time you start a session for that workspace. this behavior feels nice from a visibility perspective, but it's also a bit distracting when you're simply trying to start a new task, and you aren't even thinking about running anything yet. i wonder if it would make sense to suppress the terminal from opening for worktree setup tasks, and instead, show a spinner icon in the play button, that let's the user see that a run action is running, and then optionally open it if they want to see the details. that way there's still an entrypoint for knowing an action is running, and a way to view the logs, but without it cluttering every session.

clean_title: extension install/update fails: marketplace vspackage is received as gzip stream and then parsed as zip ( [inline code] )
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes - vs code version: 1.110.1 - os version: ubuntu 24.04.3 lts (x64) steps to reproduce: 1. launch vs code on linux x64. 2. in my environment vs code is started with these proxy variables set: - [inline code] - [inline code] - [inline code] 3. try to install or update any marketplace extension. i reproduced it with: - [inline code] - [inline code] - [inline code] 4. extension install/update fails with: - [inline code] what i found while debugging: - this is not limited to one extension; multiple unrelated extensions fail in the same way. - existing older cached vsix files on disk are valid zip files. - newly downloaded packages fail before install. - the shared process log shows repeated download failures and then [inline code] trying to open the file as zip. relevant log excerpt: [code block] i also tested the marketplace url directly outside vs code. when i download the [inline code] url normally, the response body starts with [inline code] (gzip), not [inline code] (zip). when i download the same url with http decompression enabled ( [inline code] ), the file becomes a valid vsix zip and [inline code] succeeds. that makes it look like vs code is receiving a response with [inline code] and then writing the raw gzipped body to disk instead of transparently decoding it before passing it to the zip reader. observed response headers from the marketplace url: [code block] observed raw file header without decompression: [code block] observed file header with [inline code] : [code block] possible root cause: - regression in the extension download path when handling [inline code] - possibly only on the proxy/tunneled request path i have not yet tested with insiders.

clean_title: extension update fails silently - no error message shown
clean_body: issue description when attempting to update an extension from the extensions panel, the update process fails without showing any error message to the user. screenshot as shown in the screenshot, the claude code for vs code extension displays an "update" button, but clicking it results in a silent failure with no error feedback. steps to reproduce 1. open vs code 2. navigate to the extensions panel 3. find an extension with an available update (showing "update" button) 4. click the "update" button 5. the update appears to start but then fails silently expected behavior - the extension should update successfully, or - if the update fails, a clear error message should be displayed explaining why actual behavior - the update button is clicked - no visual feedback or error message is shown - the extension remains at the old version - the "update" button continues to be displayed environment - vs code version: latest stable - os: macos (based on screenshot ui) - extension: claude code for vs code (anthropic) additional context the extensions panel shows the update is available, but clicking the update button does not complete the update process. this appears to be a general issue with the extension update mechanism rather than specific to any particular extension. suggestions 1. add proper error handling and user feedback when extension updates fail 2. show a notification or inline error message explaining the failure reason 3. provide retry options or troubleshooting steps

clean_title: extension installation fails: "end of central directory record signature not found" on both local and remote ssh
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes/no - vs code version: 1.111.0 (user setup) - os version: windows nt x64 10.0.26200 steps to reproduce: 1. open vs code (either locally or connect via remote-ssh). 2. go to the extensions view. 3. attempt to install any extension (e.g., python, claude, or even a simple theme). 4. the installation progress bar starts, then immediately throws the error: end of central directory record signature not found. either not a zip file, or file is truncated. expected behavior: the extension should be downloaded, verified, and extracted correctly. actual behavior: the zip extraction fails for every extension. this occurs consistently across different machines (local and ssh), suggesting a potential issue with how the download stream is handled or cached. remote-ssh logs: [code block] additional context: 1. i have already tried clearing the /.vscode-server directory on the remote host and the local folder, but the issue persists. 2. disk space is sufficient on both local and remote.

clean_title: stops working after macos update to 11.7.11
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes - vs code version: latest - os version: macos 11.7.11 steps to reproduce: 1. install the current .dmg from [url] or let it auto-update. 2. os error dialog opens: "you can’t use this version of the application “visual studio code” with this version of macos. you have macos 11.7.11. the application requires macos 12.0 or later." vscode worked fine prior to the os update, and the web page for the download says "macos 11.0+".

clean_title: extension auto-update fails: downloaded .vsix is gzip-compressed but not decompressed
clean_body: summary vs code fails to install/update extensions from the marketplace with the error [inline code] the root cause is that the marketplace cdn returns [inline code] files with [inline code] , but vs code's download handler does not decompress the response body, resulting in a gzip stream being written to disk instead of the expected zip archive. vs code version [code block] steps to reproduce 1. configure a socks5 or http proxy in [inline code] : [code block] (also reproducible with [inline code] ) 2. let vs code auto-update extensions (or manually install via [inline code] ). 3. observe repeated errors in the log (help → toggle developer tools → console, or window log): [code block] 4. the same extensions can be downloaded correctly using [inline code] : [code block] root cause analysis the marketplace api endpoint returns responses with the following headers: [code block] the [inline code] header indicates transport-level compression. per http/1.1 (rfc 7231 §3.1.2.2), the client must decode the content-encoding before processing the payload. vs code's extension download path (via electron/node.js) does not handle this decompression. the raw gzip-compressed bytes are written directly as the [inline code] file. since [inline code] is a zip archive, the [inline code] library then fails to parse it. verification that this is proxy-independent: [code block] affected extensions (examples) all marketplace extensions are affected. tested with: - [inline code] - [inline code] - [inline code] - [inline code] relevant log output [code block] (error repeats in a loop as vs code retries the failed downloads.) expected behavior vs code should transparently decompress [inline code] responses before writing the [inline code] file to disk, or send an [inline code] header that prevents the server from applying content-encoding to binary payloads. workaround download extensions manually using [inline code] and install via cli: [code block]

clean_title: copilot chat agent mode: toolcallingloop stop hook intermittently terminates conversations with no response
clean_body: description when using copilot chat in agent mode with claude agent sdk (claude-opus-4.6), multi-turn conversations are intermittently terminated by the toolcallingloop stop hook returning shouldcontinue=false with reasons=undefined. this causes the user to see either "sorry, no response was returned" or the conversation silently stops — even when the claude api has successfully returned a valid response. environment - vs code: 1.111.0 - copilot chat extension: 0.39.0 / 0.39.1 (both affected) - connection: vs code remote ssh (vscode-server) - model: claude-opus-4.6 (via claude copilot proxy) - copilot sku: copilot for business seat quota root cause the toolcallingloop stop hook intermittently returns shouldcontinue=false with no reason, prematurely killing the agent loop: [info] ccreq:0496329b | success | claude-opus-4.6 - claude-opus-4-6 | 2779ms | [panel/editagent] [info] [toolcallingloop] stop hook result: shouldcontinue=false, reasons=undefined claude returned a valid response, but the stop hook discarded it. the user sees "sorry, no response was returned." behavior details - intermittent: the stop hook may fire on the first message, then work fine on retry. in one session, it fired twice in a row, then worked normally — same extension host, same version. - not version-specific: observed on both 0.39.0 and 0.39.1. downgrading does not help. - agent mode only: the issue only occurs in agent mode ([panel/editagent]). when requests go through [claude copilot proxy] directly (non-agent mode), they complete successfully with no stop hook interference. - first observed: 2026-03-12. not present in 2026-03-11 logs. additional errors in logs 1. error during execution — a downstream consequence of the stop hook issue. thrown in wo.handleresultmessage (extension.js:2128:11800) after the loop is prematurely terminated. observed 9 times in a single session, triggered by rate limiting (429), vision api errors (400), and even after successful api responses. 2. pendingmigrationerror: navigator is now a global in nodejs — occurs during extension initialization on every startup. may contribute to instability. 3. operation aborted — multiple instances in remoteexthost.log, thrown from x$e.write in the extension. expected behavior 1. the stop hook should not return shouldcontinue=false when claude has returned a valid response. 2. if the stop hook decides to terminate, reasons should contain an actionable explanation, not undefined. 3. transient api errors (429 rate limiting, 400 vision not enabled) should be handled gracefully — retry or skip, not terminate the entire conversation. workaround resend the same message. it sometimes succeeds on retry. however, retry suddenly never succeeded again.

vs code version : 1.111.0 ( [hash] ) - os version : macos 26.3.1 (build 25d2128) - architecture : arm64 (apple silicon) steps to reproduce 1. open vs code 2. go to extensions panel (cmd+shift+x) 3. search for any extension (e.g., [inline code] ) 4. click install 5. error appears: "end of central directory record signature not found" expected behavior extension should install successfully. actual behavior extension installation fails with zip file error. workaround installing via command line works: [code block] additional information network tests - clean_title: add official icon support for .gx and .galaxy file
clean_body: hello vs code team, i have created a new programming language that uses the file extensions .gx and .galaxy. currently, these files appear with the default text icon in vs code, which makes them look like plain text files. to improve the developer experience, i designed a simple and clean icon: a purple curved line (like a galaxy spiral) with a small yellow dot/star in the diagonal corner. this icon visually represents the language and makes .gx and .galaxy files easier to identify in the editor. i would like to request official support for this icon in vs code’s built‑in file icon themes, so that these file types are recognized and displayed with their own unique symbol. i have already tested this icon in a custom extension, and it works well. adding it officially would help other developers who use .gx and .galaxy files to have a better experience without needing to install a separate extension. thank you for considering this request!
clean_text: add official icon support for .gx and .galaxy file hello vs code team, i have created a new programming language that uses the file extensions .gx and .galaxy. currently, these files appear with the default text icon in vs code, which makes them look like plain text files. to improve the developer experience, i designed a simple and clean icon: a purple curved line (like a galaxy spiral) with a small yellow dot/star in the diagonal corner. this icon visually represents the language and makes .gx and .galaxy files easier to identify in the editor. i would like to request official support for this icon in vs code’s built‑in file icon themes, so that these file types are recognized and displayed with their own unique symbol. i have already tested this icon in a custom extension, and it works well. adding it officially would help other developers who use .gx and .galaxy files to have a better experience without needing to install a separate extension. thank you for considering this request!

clean_body: at some point an update was made so that prompts do not fill out your chat box when you press tab to complete one. we have prompts shared between our team and we can't expect people to run other's shared prompts without being able to read them. on top of that, many prompts are setup so that you finish it out yourself, and it's hard to do that if you can't read the prompt it would be ideal if it was an option. please add the ability to turn off anything you add, as this is a terrible feature for any reasonable developer. prompts are auto-loaded from mcps and extensions, this is dangerous really. cursor does this too, and it's not usable as a developer tool.
clean_text: github copilot - add the ability to view a prompt before sending at some point an update was made so that prompts do not fill out your chat box when you press tab to complete one. we have prompts shared between our team and we can't expect people to run other's shared prompts without being able to read them. on top of that, many prompts are setup so that you finish it out yourself, and it's hard to do that if you can't read the prompt it would be ideal if it was an option. please add the ability to turn off anything you add, as this is a terrible feature for any reasonable developer. prompts are auto-loaded from mcps and extensions, this is dangerous really. cursor does this too, and it's not usable as a developer tool.

clean_title: improve screen reader labels: expose implicit context, include disable-setting hint, and label remove button
clean_body: accessibility problem some ui context is currently implicit (visual-only) and is not obvious to screen reader users. the related controls should communicate this context directly in accessible names. requested improvements 1. make the implicit context explicit in the element aria-label so screen reader users get the same meaning that sighted users infer visually. 2. include the relevant setting in the aria-label/help text so users know how to disable this behavior from settings. 3. add a clear aria-label for the remove button. why this matters when context is only conveyed visually, screen reader users can miss key meaning and available actions. including the disable-setting hint in the accessible text also improves discoverability and control. repro/observation - in the ui shown in the attached screenshot, key context appears to be implicit. - screen reader output does not sufficiently expose that context. - the remove control should have an explicit and descriptive aria-label. expected behavior - aria-label includes the missing context. - aria-label/help exposes which setting disables this behavior. - remove button has a descriptive aria-label (for example, what is being removed). notes - this request is specifically about improving accessibility semantics and discoverability for screen reader users. - screenshot referenced from report context.

clean_title: update status bar entry receives focus automatically in screen reader mode
clean_body: problem in screen reader mode, the update status bar entry appears to receive focus automatically. this unexpectedly moves focus and can interrupt the current navigation context. expected behavior the update status bar entry should not automatically receive focus in screen reader mode. actual behavior focus is moved to (or lands on) the update status bar entry without explicit user intent. why this is a problem automatic focus changes are disruptive for assistive technology users and can cause loss of context while reading or interacting with other ui elements. repro steps 1. enable screen reader mode in vs code. 2. start vs code in a state where an update status bar entry is shown. 3. observe initial focus behavior after startup / when the update entry appears. 4. focus is automatically placed on the update status bar entry. notes - this issue is specifically about behavior in screen reader mode. - the update entry should remain available and discoverable, but focus should stay where the user left it unless explicitly moved by the user.

clean_title: test: improved terminal ime composition experience with and without cli(s)
clean_body: refs: [url] - [x] macos - [x] linux - [x] windows complexity: 3 [create issue]( [url] --- thank you for testing. let's make sure people who use different languages to type also have a great experience in vs code terminal. videos in [url] have representation for previously buggy and desired/now expected behavior. please let me know if there are any questions. testing requirements - latest vs code insiders - an ime (input method editor) configured on your system for a cjk language (chinese, japanese, or korean) or any other language that uses ime (e.g., vietnamese, thai) - for windows, please set setting: [inline code] steps part 1: ime overflow fix (main focus) 1. open vs code insiders and open the integrated terminal. 2. activate your ime and begin composing text in the terminal (e.g., type a long string of pinyin for chinese, romaji for japanese, or hangul for korean). 3. while composing (before confirming/committing the text), continue typing so the composition candidate text grows long. 4. verify that the ime composition text does not overflow or extend beyond the right edge of the terminal panel. the text should be clipped to the terminal boundary. 5. as new characters are typed, the most recent input should remain visible (older composed characters scroll out of view to the left). 6. confirm the input by selecting a candidate — the finalized text should appear correctly in the terminal. 7. try composing with punctuation characters through ime as well. ensure these also do not cause overflow or cause weird behavior. part 2: ime in cli tools spawned from terminal 9. spawn a copilot cli session from the terminal and repeat the ime composition tests above — behavior should be identical with no overflow. 10. launch claude code inside the vs code integrated terminal (if available) and test ime composition. verify no overflow occurs. 11. launch codex cli inside the vs code integrated terminal (if available) and test ime composition. verify no overflow occurs. part 3: ime candidate window positioning with ghost text/placeholder text ref: [url] 12. inside one of the cli tools above (claude code, copilot cli, codex — whichever displays ghost text/placeholder text in the terminal), activate your ime. 13. when ghost text/placeholder text is visible (e.g., "try 'fix lint errors'" in claude code), start composing with ime in cjk or another ime-based language. 14. check whether the ime composition candidate window appears at the correct cursor position rather than at the end of the ghost text/placeholder text. 15. if you observe the candidate window appearing mispositioned (at the end of the ghost text instead of at the cursor), please report it.

clean_title: bug report: vs code macos - "end of central directory record signature not found"
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes - vs code version: last 3 versions (tested on all, same issue) - os version: macos productname: macos productversion: 15.7.3 buildversion: 24g419 steps to reproduce: 1. open vs code (any of the last 3 versions). 2. wait for the extension host and cloud sync initialization. 3. observe in the log the error: text error: end of central directory record signature not found. either not a zip file, or file is truncated. at n0 (...) at /node modules/yauzl/index.js:40:7 ... expected behavior: vs code should start normally without zip/extraction errors. actual behavior: vs code logs the error about “end of central directory record signature not found” and some features (like settings sync) fail to initialize properly. error seems related to the internal zip extraction of extensions or cloud sync data. additional notes: happens even when all extensions are disabled (code --disable-extensions). the error appears in the temporary apptranslocation folder on macos, which may indicate an issue with macos gatekeeper/app translocation. the problem occurs consistently across the last 3 vs code releases.

clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes - vs code version: 1.111 and 1.110 affected, 1.109 last working - os version: macos tahoe 26.3 steps to reproduce: 1. open vscode 2. open the integrated terminal 3. either open nvim through zsh or capture the keyboard shortcut events through vim [inline code] 4. press f2 then shift + 1 (or any other number) 5. you will observe that for 1.110 and 1.111 i get a wild [inline code] (b1 is changed to b5 if i click 5 for example) but for 1.109 i get the normal ascii code [inline code] if i find an easier way to reproduce this without vim i will post it. please keep in mind that this is specific to vscode integrated terminal + zsh + nvim: - i have no issues with iterm + zsh + nvim - i have no issues in the integrated terminal + nvim - i have no issues when i open a new bash terminal and then use regular vi - every other shift combination work, except [hash] i understand that this might not be a direct problem with vscode, but the integrated terminal obviously changed the way he is sending the events to the internal apps and this is breaking my day to day flow as i am unable to do [inline code] in nvim. any advice on how to debug or temporarily fix would be appreciated!

clean_title: copilot chat shows “contact your admin” for models on personal github account (vs code insiders)
clean_body: - copilot chat extension version: 0.40. [hash] - vs code version: vs code insiders 1.112.0-insider (universal) - os version: macos 15.6.1 (24g90) (apple silicon, macbook pro m4 pro) - feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): copilot chat (model selector) - selected model (e.g. gpt 4.1, claude 3.7 sonnet): claude opus 4.6 / claude sonnet 4.6 / gpt-5.4 - logs: no specific error logs appear in the output panel. (it's empty.) the issue occurs in the model selector ui where certain models show "contact your admin" despite the account being a personal github account. steps to reproduce: 1. open vs code insiders. 2. open copilot chat panel. 3. click the model selector dropdown. 4. observe that several models (claude opus 4.6, claude sonnet 4.6, gpt-5.4) appear but cannot be selected. 5. the ui displays the message "contact your admin". expected behavior: models should either be selectable or clearly indicate that they are unavailable due to plan restrictions. note: i was able to use claude opus 4.6, claude sonnet 4.6, gpt-5.4. since yesterday, i am unable to use them. actual behavior: the ui displays "contact your admin" even though the account is a personal github account (not part of an organization) with github education verification and copilot pro usage available. additional context: - the github account is a personal account , not part of any organization or enterprise workspace. - the account is github education verified and has access to copilot pro features. - premium usage quota has not been reached (approx. 68% used at the time of the screenshot). - some models appear in the dropdown but are not selectable and display the message "contact your admin". - this message is confusing because the account does not have an admin or organization controlling copilot settings. question: is this expected behavior due to plan restrictions or staged model rollout , or is the ui incorrectly showing enterprise policy messaging for personal copilot users? screenshots are attached showing the model selector and copilot usage panel.

clean_title: open terminal output as an editable text buffer
clean_body: summary add a built-in option in the vs code terminal to quickly open the current terminal output in a new editable text document within the editor. description when running programs in the integrated terminal, the output often contains logs, stack traces, or other information that developers want to inspect, search, or edit more efficiently. while the terminal supports basic search, it lacks many powerful editing and navigation features available in regular editor files. this feature would provide a quick way to convert the current terminal buffer into a normal text document inside the editor. proposed solution add an action that allows users to copy the current terminal content into a new editor tab as plain text. possible access points: - a button in the terminal ui - a command in the command palette , e.g. [inline code] - optional right-click context menu entry in the terminal when triggered: 1. the current visible or buffered terminal output is captured. 2. a new untitled text file is opened in the editor. 3. the terminal content is pasted into that file. benefits this would allow developers to immediately use full editor capabilities, including: - advanced search and replace - multi-cursor editing - code folding - better navigation - saving logs for later inspection - sharing formatted output with others example use cases - inspecting long build logs - analyzing test failures - editing copied stack traces - extracting useful parts of cli output - saving runtime logs for documentation or debugging why this matters the vs code editor provides a significantly richer text manipulation experience than the terminal view. bridging the gap between the terminal output and the editor would improve developer productivity and reduce friction when working with large or complex terminal logs. optional enhancements - option to include full terminal scrollback vs visible output only - automatically assign a language mode like [inline code] or [inline code] - option to auto-save as a log file --- in short: a simple "open terminal output in editor" action would make it much easier to analyze and work with terminal output using the full power of the vs code editor.

clean_title: read file image support (pr ) broken when model receives v1 tool schema (startline/endline)
clean_body: bug the image reading support added in [microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat]( [url] doesn't work when the model receives the v1 [inline code] tool schema (which has required [inline code] / [inline code] parameters). repro 1. open vs code insiders with copilot chat pre-release (v0.40. [hash] , which includes the merged pr) 2. in agent mode, ask the model to read an image file by path 3. the model calls [inline code] with the image path — but it must include [inline code] and [inline code] because those are required in its tool schema 4. the image handler rejects the call: [code block] root cause the pr's image detection code always rejects v1 params: [code block] - v2 schema uses [inline code] / [inline code] (optional) → model can omit them → image reading works ✅ - v1 schema uses [inline code] / [inline code] (required) → model must always provide them → [inline code] is hardcoded to [inline code] → image reading always fails ❌ some models (e.g. claude) still receive the v1 tool definition with required [inline code] / [inline code] , making image reading completely inaccessible. expected behavior [inline code] should be able to read image files regardless of which tool schema version the model receives. suggested fix either: 1. in the v1 path, ignore [inline code] / [inline code] when the file is an image (don't treat v1 as always having line params), or 2. ensure all models receive the v2 schema where [inline code] / [inline code] are optional environment - vs code insiders (march 13, 2026) - copilot chat pre-release v0.40. [hash] - model: claude (receives v1 read file schema) cc

clean_body: vs code version 1.111.0 (commit [hash] ), x64 os version windows 11 10.0.26200 x64 repro steps 1. open vs code 1.111.0 2. auto-update or run install by id for ms-python.python 3. observe failure actual extension install/update fails with: end of central directory record signature not found. either not a zip file, or file is truncated. expected extension should install/update successfully. evidence - sharedprocess.log shows repeated: - failed downloading vsix... - error while installing extension ... end of central directory... - verbose cli log shows repeated http 403 on vsix url: - ...vsixpackage?redirect=true&targetplatform=win32-x64&update=true - end get 403 - ...vsixpackage?targetplatform=win32-x64&update=true - end get 403 - manual local vsix install works, indicating install pipeline receives non-vsix payload in this path.

clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes/no - vs code version: - os version: trae cn 版本: 3.3.37 vscode 版本: 1.107.1 提交: [hash] 日期: 2026-03-11t05:50:01.273z electron: 39.2.7-release.1.26.3 (aha) node.js: 22.21.1 v8: 14.2.231.23-electron.0 os: windows nt x64 10.0.26200 构建版本: 2.3.13343 设备id: [hash] device id: [hash] steps to reproduce: c/c++ 扩展二进制文件不兼容或不匹配 c/c++ 扩展包括本机二进制文件。 通过 vs code 中的市场 ui 安装时，应包含正确的本机二进制文件。如果检测到不兼容的二进制文件，并且已通过 vs code 中的市场 ui 安装 c/c++ 扩展，则 [请报告问题]( [url] 正在重新安装 c/c++ 扩展 重新安装等效版本的扩展时，vs code 可能重用现有的扩展目录。如果要在重新安装 c/c++ 扩展时放置发生这种情况，可能需要先删除现有的扩展目录。 可在用户目录下以下路径之一的下面找到已安装的扩展目录(windows 上的 [inline code] 或 linux 和 macos 上的 [inline code] ) %userprofile%\.vscode\extensions %userprofile%\.vscode-insiders\extensions %userprofile%\.vscode-exploration\extensions 在远程连接中: $home/.vscode-server/extensions $home/.vscode-server-insiders/extensions $home/.vscode-server-exploration/extensions 已安装的 c/c++ 扩展目录的示例路径: 在 windows 上: %userprofile%\.vscode\extensions\ms-vscode.cpptools-1.9.0 在 linux 上: $home/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode.cpptools-1.9.0 然后通过 vs code 中的市场 ui 重新安装。 如果 vs code 无法部署扩展的正确版本，则可以 [已从 vs code 市场网站下载]( [url] 并使用 vs code 中市场 ui 内 "..." 菜单下的 "从 vsix... 安装" 选项安装系统的正确 vsix。

clean_title: must add [inline code] to [inline code] in system environment variables rather than user in order to build using msys2 ucrt64 [inline code]
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes/no - vs code version: [code block] - os version: [code block] - powershell version: [code block] steps to reproduce: 1. updated [inline code] installed in msys2 ucrt terminal to most recent (15.2.0) using [inline code] 2. try to build a c project using msys2 ucrt [inline code] build configuration (unchanged since before [inline code] update) and receive error [inline code] with no additional logs or indication of issue. further context: according to the documentation [using gcc with mingw]( [url] section [installing the mingw-w64 toolchain]( [url] one must add the location of the msys ucrt64's [inline code] directory (i.e. [inline code] ) to the [inline code] under the user environment variables, not the system environment variables: after updating msys2 using [inline code] , building using my [inline code] build configuration failed with error [inline code] . i verified in my command prompt that [inline code] returned the expected 15.2.0 version. however, in the vscode terminal, the version returned was the version associated with my old mingw32 version (i.e. 9.2.0). it seems that the default terminal is powershell rather than the command prompt, and that by default powershell falls back to the system [inline code] environment variable. as soon as i added [inline code] to the top of the system [inline code] environment variable, the build was successful. this is in spite of explicitly setting the build configuration command [inline code] in my [inline code] : [code block] i am unsure as to whether this is a bug with vscode (i.e. default terminal configuration), a bug with the c/c++ vscode extension, or simply requires an update to the documentation; i haven't been able to find an existing bug report on this repo nor on the [ [inline code] ]( [url] repo.

clean_title: agent enters degenerate text-generation loop when editing .prompt.md files containing backtick fences
clean_body: - copilot chat extension version: 0.37.9 - vs code version: 1.109.5 - os version: windows server 10.0.20348 (windows server 2022) - feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode - selected model (e.g. gpt 4.1, claude 3.7 sonnet): claude opus 4.6 - logs: (see attached - i hope!) steps to reproduce: (as self reported my friend claude herself) repro steps: ask copilot agent to edit a .prompt.md file that contains triple-backtick code fences as content expected: normal file edit actual: agent enters infinite text-generation loop (not a tool-calling loop) with alternating backtick markers and repeated phrases evidence: three separate occurrences in one session (you can reference the line numbers) root cause hypothesis: the read file tool wraps .prompt.md content in backtick fences with prompt as the language identifier, creating ambiguity when the file content itself contains backtick fences. the model cannot distinguish content from framing. attach (or excerpt) the relevant sections of exhibit c as evidence [exhibit c.md]( [url] see lines 281, 1309, 1319 | line | trigger | loop pattern -- | -- | -- | -- 1 | 281 | editing 04-end-of-day.prompt.md (replacement attempt) | "markers are part of the read file display formatting..." oscillating with [code block] markers 2 | 1309 | re-reading 04-end-of-day.prompt.md for the full rewrite | same root cause, second occurrence in the same file 3 | 1319 | editing 14-end-session.prompt.md | "fence structure:" oscillating with [code block] markers — the same class of bug but a different trigger file

clean_title: allow github account preferences per profile for extensions (e.g. github copilot)
clean_body: type: feature request vs code version: 1.110.1 (commit [hash] , x64) os: ubuntu 24.04.2 lts, kernel 6.17.0-14-generic relevant extensions installed: github.copilot (built-in via vs code) github.copilot-chat vscode.github-authentication (built-in) feature request: currently, the preferred github account assigned to an extension is stored globally and shared across all profiles. switching profiles does not change the active account used by extensions like github copilot. expected behavior: each vs code profile should store its own preferred github account per extension, so switching profiles automatically uses the account associated with that profile — without manual intervention via "manage extension account preferences". example use case: profile default → github account personal-user → personal copilot license profile work → github account work-user → corporate copilot license reproducible steps: sign in to vs code with two different github accounts (e.g. user-a and user-b), both having a copilot license. create two profiles: default and work. in profile default, open the account menu (bottom-left) → "manage extension account preferences" → assign user-a to github copilot and github copilot chat. switch to profile work → open the account menu → "manage extension account preferences". assign user-b to github copilot and github copilot chat. switch back to profile default. what you expect to see: copilot uses user-a automatically in profile default. what you actually see: copilot still uses user-b (the last account set), regardless of the active profile. the account preference is not persisted per profile. root cause (investigated): the key github.copilot-github is stored in the global /.config/code/user/globalstorage/state.vscdb, not in the per-profile database at /.config/code/user/profiles/ /globalstorage/state.vscdb. vs code does not read or write this preference from the profile-scoped database when switching profiles. current workaround: the user must manually reassign the preferred account every time they switch profiles, which defeats the purpose of profiles for multi-identity workflows. why this matters: profiles are designed to provide isolated, context-specific environments. authentication preferences for extensions are a natural part of that context. without per-profile account binding, users with multiple github identities (e.g. personal + enterprise copilot licenses) cannot benefit from profiles for account isolation. dev tools console errors: none — this is a missing feature, not an error condition. vs code version: code 1.110.1 ( [hash] , 2026-03-06t23:03:27.520z) os version: linux x64 6.17.0-14-generic snap modes:

clean_body: bug the [inline code] field in byok model configuration (e.g., [inline code] ) is accepted by the schema but has no effect on which edit tools are exposed to the model. root cause the plumbing between [inline code] and [inline code] is broken: 1. [inline code] (in [inline code] ) defines [inline code] 2. [inline code] (same file) converts capabilities to [inline code] but drops [inline code] — it's not mapped to any field 3. [inline code] (in [inline code] ) reads edit tools from [inline code] — a vs code api capability 4. [inline code] checks [inline code] first, but it's always empty for byok models, so it falls through to hardcoded name matching or the learning state machine expected behavior setting [inline code] in a byok model config should cause the model to use [inline code] instead of the default [inline code] . actual behavior [inline code] is silently ignored. the edit tool is determined by: 1. hardcoded model name matching ( [inline code] — names containing 'gpt'/'openai' → applypatch, 'sonnet' → replacestring) 2. the learning state machine (defaults to replacestring for unknown models) suggested fix either: - map [inline code] through to [inline code] during byok model registration so [inline code] picks it up - or have [inline code] include [inline code] in a field that [inline code] can read from the endpoint reproduction [code block] the model will still get [inline code] / [inline code] instead of [inline code] . workaround name the model to include 'gpt' or 'openai' to trigger the hardcoded preference in [inline code] . references (in microsoft/vscode-copilot-chat) - [inline code] — [inline code] and [inline code] - [inline code] — [inline code] from [inline code] - [inline code] — [inline code] and [inline code]

clean_body: refs: feature [url] pr [url] - [x] anyos - [x] anyos complexity: 3 authors: [create issue]( [url] --- this tpi covers the zoom feature in integrated browser. with this feature, the user will be able to change the zoom level of the browser independently from the zoom level of vs code itself. when you zoom in/out on a particular page, the zoom level is remembered for that website (host). as for the default zoom level, it will match the current vs code zoom level, but you can change that default in settings to be a specific, fixed zoom level value if needed. setup tips: to open a browser tab option 1: run the command "browser: open integrated browser" option 2: from an existing browser tab, do "new tab" or [inline code] + [inline code] option 3: ask the agent to open a website in the integrated browser. note that the behavior will be slightly different depending on the value of "workbench.browser.enablechattools", which enables agentic tools. to access browser settings, click on "browser settings" on the overflow menu to the right of url bar scenarios to cover: play around with the zoom feature in integrated browser and make sure that it works as you would expect of such a feature in a regular web browser. open edge and/or chrome on the side and compare the functionality. entrypoints (only work when integrated browser has focus): menu: "zoom in", "zoom out", and "reset zoom" in overflow menu to the right of url bar keyboard shortcuts: zoom in: [inline code] + [inline code] or [inline code] + [inline code] + [inline code] zoom out: [inline code] + [inline code] or [inline code] + [inline code] + [inline code] (the version with [inline code] is disabled on linux to match vs code shortcuts) reset zoom: [inline code] + [inline code] (this is num pad 0, not the regular 0 key, to match vs code shortcuts and avoid conflict with focus sidebar) setting: [inline code] sets the default zoom level. if unset, this will be "match window". some things to try: try multiple tabs and multiple windows. tabs with same vs. with different host (i.e., all pages from the same subdomain should share the same zoom level) changing the zoom level of vs code itself restarting vs code regular tabs vs. ephemeral tabs

clean_title: allow updating a system/admin vs code install from within vs code
clean_body: type: feature request tl;dr on windows systems where uac is disabled (enablelua = 0), all processes run elevated. in this configuration: the vs code user installer cannot run because it detects elevation. existing system installer installations cannot update automatically. this leaves manual reinstallation of the system installer as the only way to update vs code. it would be helpful if vs code supported updating system installations via an elevation prompt, allowing users in uac-disabled environments to update from within vs code rather than downloading the installer manually. ---------------------------------------------------------------------- environment windows 11 domain-joined machine vs code originally installed using the system installer uac disabled (enablelua = 0) user account is a domain user, not the built-in administrator problem when attempting to update vs code to the latest version, the built-in update mechanism does not work because the installation was performed using the system installer. the obvious alternative is to download and run the user installer, which normally allows updates without administrator privileges. however, when uac is disabled (enablelua = 0), all processes run with elevated privileges. as a result: the user installer refuses to run, displaying: “this user installer is not meant to be run as an administrator.” because uac is disabled, it is not possible to launch a non-elevated process. this means: the user installer cannot run the system installer cannot update automatically the only option is to manually download and reinstall the system installer each time observed behaviour existing vs code install uses the system installer auto-update does not apply because elevation is required attempting to run the user installer fails because the process is detected as elevated disabling elevation is impossible when enablelua = 0 result users in environments where uac is disabled cannot use the vs code user installer and also cannot update a system installer installation from within vs code. updates therefore require manually downloading and reinstalling vs code each time. suggested improvement possible improvements that would resolve this scenario: allow the user installer to run when elevated if the target install is in the user profile. allow vs code to download and apply updates to a system install by prompting for elevation. provide an “update with elevation” option inside vs code when the installation type requires it. why this matters in corporate or domain environments where uac is disabled or restricted by policy, the current behaviour makes updating vs code unnecessarily difficult. allowing an elevation prompt for updates (similar to many other windows applications) would make updates straightforward regardless of installation type. vs code version: code 1.106.3 ( [hash] , 2025-11-25t22:28:18.024z) os version: windows nt x64 10.0.26100 modes:

clean_title: "generate agent instructions" consumed 100% of github copilot free chat message quota in minutes
clean_body: the "generate agent instructions" feature just consumed 100% of my chat message quota in a matter of minutes. my allowance reset earlier today (as evidenced by "allowance resets april 11, 2026."), and this was the first query i ran today. it ran for just a few minutes, using the grok code fast 1 model, then showed the message "you've reached your monthly chat messages quota. upgrade to copilot pro (30-day free trial) or wait for your allowance to renew.". of course, i have done this many times before, and never had the issue before, so i would imagine that this is a rare occurence, but i don't think it should be possible to consume tokens so quickly without at least prompting for user interaction. does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes - vs code version: 1.109.5 - os version: windows 11 enterprise 25h2 build 26200.7840 (windows feature experience pack 1000.26100.291.0) steps to reproduce: 1. open a new chat window 2. click "generate agent instructions" 3. wait

clean_body: type: bug as of 11/03/2026, the agent mode is unable to edit jupyter notebooks. the chat tool thinks it is doing edits, but they just don't go through and it gets stuck in a loop. plan the refactor is centered in 03 continuous colocation.ipynb. i verified that the current hdr helper cell contains duplicate apply hdr s curve and render point overlay definitions, so part of the current behavior is coming from accidental override rather than an explicit rendering design. the plan below fixes that first, then makes preview/export consistent, then documents the whole notebook. steps consolidate the duplicated hdr helper functions into one canonical rendering pipeline in 03 continuous colocation.ipynb. add a user-facing colormap mode layer with exactly these options: fire, red, blue, green, gray on black, gray on white. map those modes to the requested palettes and styles: cc.fire, cc.kr, cc.kb, cc.kg, cc.gray, plus the current gray-on-white variant. separate tone mapping from style selection so the same hdr transform can render on black or white backgrounds with the appropriate point color. refactor show hdr plot to accept the colormap mode explicitly and make it the single source of truth for rendering. simplify render hdr figure from settings so it just calls the same rendering logic used in preview and saves that image to disk, using the already-built 2000px agg data objects instead of rebuilding higher-resolution canvases. update the settings dictionaries, printed settings, widget extraction, and export cells so they include the new colormap field and no longer imply resolution-invariant behavior. keep the current exported outputs on gray on white with the same midpoint, contrast, and point settings you already have, so the current deliverables stay visually consistent. add a colormap selector to the interactive preview panel for cosine, co-location, and dispersion graphs. add systematic markdown and concise inline comments across the full notebook so each phase is clearly documented: loading, merges/spatial join, embedding normalization, clustering/layout, graph construction, aggregate creation, hdr rendering, calibration, and export. clean up the notebook narrative so the distinction between precomputed graph inputs and display-only cells is explicit. verify that previewed settings now match saved output because both paths use the same precomputed aggregate arrays. verification re-run the helper and plotting cells and confirm the three current graphs still render under gray on white. check all six colormap modes on at least one graph. print a settings dictionary from the widget, paste it into an export cell, and confirm the saved image matches the preview. re-run the existing export cells and confirm they still write to the same output locations. scope decisions included: full-notebook documentation pass. included: hdr helper refactor, preview/export consistency, and new colormap options. excluded: any change to the underlying graph construction or statistical logic. recommended cleanup: remove stale scale / resolution fields if they become dead after the export refactor. i’m going to inspect the notebook structure and the hdr-related cells first, then i’ll refactor those cells so preview and export share one rendering path and add the new colormap mode end-to-end. i found the hdr/helper and preview-export region; next i’ll read those exact notebook sections to refactor safely without touching graph-construction logic. i’ve identified all target rendering cells; now i’ll scan the earlier notebook sections to add the requested phase-by-phase documentation without changing analytical logic. i’m now applying the core refactor: one canonical hdr pipeline, explicit colormap modes, and a shared preview/export render function that uses the existing precomputed aggregates. core helper refactor is in place. next i’m wiring every plotting, widget, and export cell to include the new colormap field and remove stale scale/size settings so preview and saved outputs match. planning parallel cell updates planning systematic markdown updates i’m doing a documentation pass now: adding phase headings and clarifying where computation ends and display-only rendering begins, including the preview/export consistency note. i’m doing a consistency sweep now to remove remaining stale resolution fields and any old scale usage, then i’ll run a quick validation check on the notebook json. i spotted an inconsistency: the file readback still shows old snippets, so i’m re-checking notebook cell metadata/order before applying the remaining updates with exact targeting. the notebook edit api didn’t persist changes to disk, so i’m switching to direct json patching of the notebook file and will update each target cell explicitly. i’m applying one consolidated patch to the notebook json now so all helper, preview, export, and documentation updates land consistently in the on-disk file. verifying results with grep read 03 continuous colocation.ipynb, lines 680 to 980 read 03 continuous colocation.ipynb, lines 980 to 1357 verifying patch effect with git diff read changed files in the active git repository testing patch path formats i confirmed no on-disk changes were written yet, so i’m retrying with workspace-relative patch paths to ensure edits persist in git-tracked files. using "apply patch" listing project root read madridamenities testing absolute path format preparing patch with absolute paths read 03 continuous colocation.ipynb, lines 450 to 470 verifying patch effect on repo files read changed files in the active git repository confirming notebook edit approach i’m proceeding with direct notebook-cell edits again (the only safe notebook editing path here), focusing first on the hdr helper and settings cells that drive both preview and export behavior. rendering and settings propagation is now wired through the same apis. i’m finishing widget extraction, export dictionaries, and markdown narrative cleanup to match your scope exactly. extension version: 0.39.0 vs code version: code 1.111.0 ( [hash] , 2026-03-06t23:06:10z) clean_title: copilot is ruined, and so is vscode

clean_title: copilot chat uses wrong account despite setting extension account preference
clean_body: - copilot chat extension version: 0.36.2 - vs code version: 1.108.2 (universal) - os version: darwin arm64 25.2.0 - feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode - selected model (e.g. gpt 4.1, claude 3.7 sonnet): claude opus 4.6 - logs: i have a private github account and my github enterprise(?) account in the account list of vscode. the org account is just there to give me copilot access, it does not allow me to do e.g. work on github at all. this is where the private github account comes in. i also have a copilot subscription on my private account. changing the account preference of the chat extension does change the [inline code] button in the bottom right corner. however it apparently does not change the account the extension uses. i was basically burning through my private github account copilot requests, instead of the one that i should use (from the org), despite vscode showing me the usage for the org account, leading to a [inline code] (and even exceeding that because i had premium requests enabled for some other stuff...) there seems to be a bug in the vscode copilot extension regarding this account selection.

clean_title: copilot chat: repeated "token expired or invalid" 401 errors requiring restart
clean_body: bug description copilot chat intermittently fails with a 401 "token expired or invalid" error. this has occurred twice within a 3-hour window. the error persists on retry — clicking "try again" returns the same 401. error details [code block] does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled? no — this is specific to the github copilot chat extension. - vs code version: latest stable (march 2026) - - os version: macos 15 (darwin 25.3.0) steps to reproduce 1. use copilot chat normally in vs code 2. 2. after some period of use (variable, within 1-3 hours), the next chat request fails with 401 "token expired or invalid" 3. 3. clicking "try again" does not resolve the error — it returns the same 401 4. 4. the only workaround is to reload vs code or sign out/sign back in to github expected behavior copilot should automatically refresh its authentication token before it expires, or gracefully re-authenticate when a 401 is received, rather than entering an unrecoverable error state. actual behavior the token silently expires and copilot does not attempt to refresh it. every subsequent request (including retries) fails with the same 401 until the user manually intervenes. additional context - this happened twice in 3 hours on the same session - - signing out and back in to github resolves the issue temporarily - - - reloading vs code ( [inline code] ) also works as a workaround - - - - the token refresh mechanism appears to be broken or not triggering reliably

clean_title: github copilot extension v0.39.0 incompatible with vs code 1.111
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes/no - na as this is issue with vs code to copilot only. after updating to the latest version of vs code (1.111), the github copilot extension v0.39.0 becomes incompatible and is disabled by the editor. vs code reports that the extension is not compatible with the current version of the editor even though both components are the latest available versions at the time of writing. this prevents using copilot functionality such as inline suggestions and copilot chat in the editor. - copilot chat extension version: 0.39.0 - vs code version: 1.111.0 - os version: windows nt x64 10.0.26100 steps to reproduce: 1. install or update to vs code 1.111 2. install github copilot extension v0.39.0 3. restart vs code if prompted 4. open the extensions view 2. it doesn't anymore now, with message invalid extension detected expected behavior github copilot extension should load normally and provide inline suggestions and copilot chat functionality. actual behavior vs code shows that the extension is incompatible with the current version and disables it automatically. as a result: copilot suggestions are not available copilot chat cannot be used extension appears disabled due to incompatibility additional context this issue appears to occur immediately after updating vs code to version 1.111 with copilot extension version 0.39.0 installed.

clean_title: vs code becomes unresponsive while github copilot chat generates multi-file patches
clean_body: type: bug type: bug when using github copilot chat in vs code to perform multi-file refactoring/editing, the entire vs code window becomes heavily unresponsive while copilot is generating or applying patches. this is not limited to the edited file. the whole ide is affected: - editor becomes sluggish or freezes - terminal responsiveness is degraded - sidebar/tree view becomes slow - general ui interaction is delayed while copilot is processing changes steps to reproduce 1. open a project in vs code 2. open github copilot chat 3. ask copilot to refactor or update multiple files/components 4. let copilot start generating patches / applying edits across files 5. while it is in states such as "generating patch", "processing", or similar, try interacting with: - editor - terminal - explorer/sidebar - other open files actual result the whole vs code window becomes unresponsive or very slow during copilot's patch generation/editing workflow. expected result copilot processing should not degrade the responsiveness of the entire ide window. even if patch generation is heavy, it should be isolated enough that the editor, terminal, and general ui remain usable. additional context this does not appear to be a general vs code, project, or hardware limitation in my case. i can reproduce this in the same project , on the same pc , in the same vs code environment , with no other heavy workloads running . for comparison, when using the openai codex extension locally in this same project and on this same machine, this ide-wide unresponsiveness does not happen. code can be generated and files can be edited without causing the whole vs code window to become sluggish or freeze. this strongly suggests the problem is specific to github copilot chat's patch generation/editing workflow, rather than a general issue with the project itself or my machine. the problem is especially noticeable during larger multi-file edits/refactors. notes this feels like copilot's background processing is impacting the responsiveness of the full vs code window too aggressively. even if the feature is computationally expensive, it should not make the whole ide stall just because it is editing files. please let me know if logs, performance traces, or a minimal repro workflow would help. extension version: 0.39.0 vs code version: code 1.111.0 ( [hash] , clean_title: localization process isn't aligned with weekly releases
clean_body: due to the process change of weekly releases, the process for localization of vs code is broken. a lot of it heavily relied on monthly branching: [url] even in our previous process monthly release process, the branching was done by endgame champ. additionally, the pipeline relies on building vs code in order to extract the strings from it... with vs code moving faster, keeping those build steps stable will be difficult and has already given me headaches over the years. with weekly releases this has to be rethought. ideas: can we not do this branch swapping and run the pipeline off of either the current release branch or even main? (this will need to make sure that the engine version is properly reflected in the resulting language packs) can we avoid building vscode in a satellite repo and instead bring this creation of english strings into core and then pass them to a pipeline in vscode-loc-drop? since we are in a broken state today, this is quite important to address as it means no new strings are translated.
clean_text: localization process isn't aligned with weekly releases due to the process change of weekly releases, the process for localization of vs code is broken. a lot of it heavily relied on monthly branching: [url] even in our previous process monthly release process, the branching was done by endgame champ. additionally, the pipeline relies on building vs code in order to extract the strings from it... with vs code moving faster, keeping those build steps stable will be difficult and has already given me headaches over the years. with weekly releases this has to be rethought. ideas: can we not do this branch swapping and run the pipeline off of either the current release branch or even main? (this will need to make sure that the engine version is properly reflected in the resulting language packs) can we avoid building vscode in a satellite repo and instead bring this creation of english strings into core and then pass them to a pipeline in vscode-loc-drop? since we are in a broken state today, this is quite important to address as it means no new strings are translated.

clean_title: bug? integrated terminal font rendering appears thin / poorly antialiased in light themes on macos
clean_body: does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: yes/no type: bug platform: macos vs code version: 1.111.0 (observed in recent updates) environment: occurs across multiple macbook devices with different screen resolutions description in recent versions of vs code, the integrated terminal appears to have a font rendering issue when using light themes on macos . when both the editor theme and terminal theme are light , terminal fonts appear overly thin with visible pixel zig-zag artifacts , suggesting that font antialiasing is not being applied correctly. this significantly reduces readability. this behavior does not occur when: - using dark editor themes - using a light editor theme with a dark terminal theme the issue appears to affect all light themes tested , not just a specific theme. observed effects - terminal text appears very thin and jagged - antialiasing artifacts are visible - powerline / nerd fonts icons become difficult to distinguish - tui box-drawing characters become hard to read examples: - the frame around the “openai codex” banner in the screenshot becomes almost illegible. - similar rendering artifacts appear in other terminal-based tools that rely on text ui elements. ![image]( [url] ![image]( [url] steps to reproduce 1. use macos 2. install the latest version of vs code 3. select a light editor theme 4. use a light terminal theme 5. open the integrated terminal 6. use a terminal font such as nerd fonts / powerline fonts 7. run a tui application (example: codex cli) expected behavior terminal fonts should render with proper antialiasing and normal stroke thickness , consistent with how they appear: - in dark vs code themes - in native macos terminal or iterm - in vs code when using dark terminal themes troubleshooting attempted the following were tested but did not resolve the issue : - disabling macos system font antialiasing - enabling or disabling terminal font weight settings - setting the following in [inline code] : [code block] - testing across multiple macbook devices - testing across different display resolutions - testing with multiple light themes [code block]

clean_title: render some distinction with default approvals when using copilot cli worktree
clean_body: - select copilot cli as agent type, worktree as isolation mode, and default approvals - ask the copilot cli to edit package.json - ❓ copilot cli makes the edit without asking for permission, since it's working on a worktree - if i try the same flow with workspace instead of worktree, or with the local agent instead of copilot cli, then i get prompted as expected with default approvals selected should we provide some sort of indicator to users somewhere about this behavior? right now, the explanation for approvals mentions using my configured settings - which settings? and are these settings different for different agent harnesses?

clean_title: github copilot consumed tokens during loop and request timeout
clean_body: type: performance issue i experienced an issue with github copilot on 10th march between 6:30 pm – 7:05 pm. during this period: initially, copilot got stuck in a loop of “editing → analysing → processing” while working on files. on subsequent attempts, it did not respond. the last attempt resulted in the following error message: sorry, your request failed. please try again. request failed: 408 {"error":{"message":"timed out reading request body. try again, or use a smaller request size.","code":"user request timeout"}} despite these failures, my tokens were still consumed, which doesn’t seem correct since the requests didn’t successfully complete. extension version: 0.38.2 vs code version: code 1.110.1 ( [hash] , 2026-03-06t23:03:27.520z) os version: windows nt x64 10.0.19045 modes:

clean_title: importerror: dll load failed for pywrap tensorflow internal when using tensorflow via jep in java app
